name: Junada ibn Ka'b
title: The Epic Father
shortDescription: A sincere Shia and companion of the Prophet (PBUH) who attended Ashura with his wife and 11-year-old son, and both he and his son were martyred.
fullStory:
Junada ibn Ka'b al-Ansari was a prominent Shia from the Khazraj tribe. He had the honor of meeting the Prophet (PBUH) and participating in the early battles of Islam. Junada was also a loyal companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). His love for the Ahl al-Bayt was so deep that he, along with his wife Bahriyya bint Mas'ud and his young son 'Amr', joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan from Mecca.

On the journey from Mecca to Karbala, Junada was constantly in the service of the Imam and spared no effort. His family's presence on this perilous journey demonstrated their insight and complete readiness for sacrifice.

On the morning of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd ordered the first mass attack and a rain of arrows poured towards Imam Hussain's (AS) tents, Junada ibn Ka'b was in the front line of defense. He stood bravely against the enemy's assault.

In this cowardly attack, which led to the martyrdom of over 50 of the Imam's companions, Junada was severely wounded by the swords and spears of the Kufans and attained the grace of martyrdom.

After his martyrdom, his brave wife dressed their 11-year-old son (Amr) in battle armor and sent him to the battlefield to continue his father's path. The martyrdom of Junada and his son is an unparalleled tableau of a family's sacrifice in the path of Wilayah.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى جُنادَةَ بْنِ كَعْبٍ الأَنْصارِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Junada ibn Ka'b al-Ansari.
